programmes.show_description: Paramedics help a woman who's had a panic attack while driving in rush-hour traffic. And an 11-year-old boy dials 999 when his mum collapses and is unresponsive.
This episode focuses on how people deal with physical and emotional pain. Dad-of-four Simon hasn't eaten for days after his partner ended their relationship.
A look at the challenges of motherhood and the responsibility of raising sons, meeting mothers with concerns about their own health and that of their babies and adult sons.
Horse-mad Louise plans a day of riding on the final day in Porto. She also books a photographer, but will her fellow holidaymakers want any mementoes as the judging commences? (Ep10/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Alistair and Emma host at their four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse in sleepy Lynton, north Devon. The group activity requires a head for heights, and Mrs Tee proves hard to please.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the sunny Isle of Wight, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find an unusual three-wheeled motorbike and unearth an old projector screen that Simon wants to turn into a work of art.
Lorraine and Jon want to fulfil their long-held dream of owning a house in the whitewashed mountain villages of Axarquía in Spain. Jean Johansson shows them what their £110,000 can buy.
West Midlands. Sue promises an evening her guests will never forget. But that might be for the wrong reasons, as her starter reminds one guest of baby food and another of toilets... (Ep1/5)
West Midlands. Business guru Marc plans a Frank Sinatra-inspired night of swing and classic American dining. But can he impress his guests after annoying them on the previous night? (Ep2/5)
West Midlands. Teaching assistant Kim hosts a summer holiday-themed evening, with a Greek menu and her favourite game: 'cheeky darts'. But will her night hit the bullseye? (Ep3/5)
West Midlands. College manager and part-time comedian Paul turns his home into a 'Black Country boozer' as he attempts to pull in the points as well as pulling pints. (Ep4/5)
West Midlands. Mole catcher Michael hopes to impress with his fancy French cuisine. But one of his guests vomits before they've even started eating! (Ep5/5)
At the Brook Meadow B&B, near Market Harborough, interior designer Claire prides herself on her dazzling décor. Is it a hit with her guests?
James hosts the second visit of the week at The Apollo Inn, in Barlborough, Derbyshire. He thinks his poached eggs are the best in the business, but will his guests egg-ree?
The B&Bers head to The Swan Inn, in Herefordshire, run by best friends Luke and Heather. The guests are wowed by how sparklingly new everything is but the continental breakfast isn't a hit.
The week's final visit is to White Mark Glamping in Watlington, Oxfordshire, where hosts Steph and Martin are certain the B&Bers will love the great outdoors. Their guests aren't so sure...
It's payment day, and time for some score settling. One host comes under fire for the harsh criticisms they left throughout the week, and things get emotional before the winner is revealed.
Bedford Ice Cream Van: Can Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend restore Barbara's treasured but decaying 1978 Bedford HA ice cream van to its former glory?
Ford Mk3 Cortina: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a 1973 Mk3 Ford Cortina, once Britain's bestselling car but now something of a rarity.
Kirstie Allsopp helps Stuart and Lara, who live 100 miles apart, to find a home in Buckinghamshire. Phil Spencer takes Randhir and Sheetal on an urgent search in Berkshire.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: We head to Cambridgeshire for one of the show's more turbulent episodes. A fitness firm and tarantula collection feature in the hunt for a more family-friendly home.
This breathtaking series uses state-of-the-art drone photography to explore Britain's country estates, beginning with Blenheim Palace and its gardens and woodland. (Ep1/4)
Kevin, 57, has fallen off a ladder and landed on a sharp metal bar. John, 53, has fallen down a flight of steps. Ross, 31, has fallen and hit his head while on a night out.
Graham, who's 50, is rushed to King's after having a suspected heart attack. Pat, who's 71, is brought in after her husband Bill found her unconscious at home.
This breathtaking series uses state-of-the-art drone photography to explore Britain's country estates, beginning with Blenheim Palace and its gardens and woodland. (Ep1/4)
Kevin, 57, has fallen off a ladder and landed on a sharp metal bar. John, 53, has fallen down a flight of steps. Ross, 31, has fallen and hit his head while on a night out.
Graham, who's 50, is rushed to King's after having a suspected heart attack. Pat, who's 71, is brought in after her husband Bill found her unconscious at home.
On day one in Barcelona, Damon treats his fellow holidaymakers to a Segway tour and dinner at an Alice in Wonderland-themed restaurant. Is it a fairy-tale start to the week? (Ep11/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The competition heads north to the three-star Vidella in Blackpool, run by fun-loving pranksters John and Neil, who have hatched a plan to pay back Alistair for hating their home town.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Cockermouth, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet blacksmith Jon, who spent many years working on fairgrounds: can the boys find some spooky rides in his shed?
Lorna and Gordon are relocating to the beautiful town of Benalmádena on Spain's Costa del Sol. With a budget of £250k, can Ben Hillman find their perfect place with plenty of space?
Hastings and Rye. Nursery owner Louise plans a Thai menu. But one of her guests is a self-professed Thai food expert, and a power cut threatens to ruin her night before it's begun. (Ep1/5)
Hastings and Rye. Horse-mad Emily hosts a wholesome evening of comfort food. The night starts with a surprise new guest: Irish crooner Dec, who was in The Bachelors in the 1960s. (Ep2/5)
Hastings and Rye. Flamboyant mature student Gary's fun-filled evening features fancy dress, diabolical dancing, fits of giggles and some tactless table manners. (Ep3/5)
Hastings and Rye. Former pop star Dec plans some musical surprises and a simple seaside menu, on a night that features 'talking' olives, celebrity stories and a peculiar pudding. (Ep4/5)
Hastings and Rye. Beauty therapist Chiara goes back to her Italian roots. But Dec compares his host to a Page Three glamour girl, and Gary gets in a fluster in a game with Emily. (Ep5/5)
The first visit of the week is to The Lazy Pug in Warwickshire, run by best friends Ash and Rosie, where the quirky décor and neon lighting give some of the guests a headache.
The B&Bers head to Cardiff to stay at Crofts Hotel, run by chef Lorraine. They're all expecting a knockout breakfast, but uninvited guests ruin things for some of them.
Anthony hosts the week's third visit at The Hollybush Inn, in Warwickshire. His 16th-century coaching inn wows the guests with its history. But some think the cleanliness needs updating.
The B&Bers go glamping in the week's last visit, at The Pretty Thing glampsite in Essex. Ash and Rosie embrace the luxe of their Aztec tent before host Phil serves his first-ever breakfast.
It's payment day, and the B&Bers reconvene to feud about facilities, bicker over beds and cross swords over sausages, before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Bond Bug: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend enter the weird and wonderful world of the Bond Bug, a heroic three-wheel automotive flop.
Fiat Uno Turbo: In Wellingborough, Tim and Fuzz fix up one of the fastest hatchbacks of the 80s. Owner Gerry's ill health has meant that his Uno has been left to rot on his drive.
Kirstie Allsopp helps Andy and Jenny search for their first home near Warrington. Phil Spencer is with Margot, who hopes to find a forever home in the seaside town of Crosby.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In the village of Clifford, Kirstie and Phil meet Vic and Paul. From the outside, their idyllic home looks perfect for a family, but appearances can be deceptive.
In West Yorkshire's rolling hills, Kara and Jonny build a striking stone and zinc home. The challenges come in thick and fast, but can they pull it off and redefine accessible design?
Alyce, who's 89, is brought to King's after falling near her home. Sean, who's 23, is rushed in after having a seizure that's lasted most of the night.
This episode shows how a trip to A&E can act as a long-overdue wake-up call. Phil, 36, has chronic pain in his back and side. Kamilla, 12, has been knocked down by a car.
